summary: Closed, unmerged PR focused on the trophy list module `src/melee/ty/tylist.c` and related `ty` headers. It attempted to improve decompilation/readability by typing `TyListArg`/`TyListState` fields, adding small local structs for archive/camera/display state, replacing raw JObj translation/dirty-flag code with HSD setter calls, converting string literals to offsets into existing string data, and implementing `un_80313774`. The decomp.dev bot showed a small net matched-code gain (+0.02%, +792 bytes) with new 100% matches for `un_8031263C` and `un_803124BC`, but also several significant regressions in other `tylist` functions, which likely made the PR unsuitable as-is. There were no human review comments in the slice.

key_files.change: Major rewrite/refactor of trophy list decompilation; added local structs, typed function bodies, implemented `un_80313774`, adjusted archive symbol loading, text positioning, list scrolling, camera/scissor setup, and JObj animation/translation handling.
Refined `TyListArg` layout from individually named raw fields to grouped `links[3]`, `jobjs[3]`, and `texts[3]`; adjusted signedness and padding in `TyListArg`/`TyListState`.
Updated prototypes to use `TyListArg*` and `TyListState*`; changed `un_8031305C` signature and removed public declaration of local/static `fn_80313BD8`.
Formatting-only change to compound literal style for `GXColor` casts.
Formatting-only change to function pointer cast spacing.
Formatting-only change to function pointer cast spacing.

what_changed: `un_803124BC` and `un_8031263C` changed archive/file/symbol string arguments from direct string literals such as `"TyDatai.dat"`, `"TyDatai.usd"`, `"tyInitModelTbl"`, etc. to offsets from `un_803FDD18` such as `data + 0x608`, `data + 0x9DC`, and related offsets; bot reported both became 100% matched.
Added local helper/overlay structs in `tylist.c`: `TyListArchiveAnimData`, `TyListWaitData`, `TyListCameraState`, `TyListDispEntry`, and `TyListDispState`.
Changed `un_80312904` from a `void*` row argument and local duplicate `TyListRow` struct to `TyListArg*`, using `jobjs[0]` and `texts[0..2]` for trophy list text placement and colors.

Reworked `un_80312BAC` to iterate typed `TyListArg` entries via `links[1]`, set selected/list indices, call `un_80312904`/`un_80313464`, use `HSD_JObjSetTranslateY`, and drive archive animation jobjs through `archive->jobjs[i]`.
Split repeated JObj translation update logic around `un_80312E88` into an inline helper `un_80312E88_inline`, replacing raw translation writes and dirty flag blocks with `HSD_JObjSetTranslateY`.

Implemented previously stubbed `un_80313774`, which bot reported improved from 0.00% to 61.66%. The diff shows it initializes `TyListState`, sets entry counts, links list entries, creates display/text/JObj objects, and configures camera/scissor/text resources.
Simplified raw JObj translation setter code in `un_80313508` by using `HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z` instead of expanded assert/dirty-flag sequences.
Adjusted `un_803147C4` to use byte offsets from `un_804A2AC0` for state clearing and light-object gobj storage, rather than fully typed state members, apparently for matching/codegen.
Minor unrelated formatting updates changed cast spacing for `GXColor` and function-pointer casts in gm/gr files.
smart_moves: Using grouped arrays (`links[3]`, `jobjs[3]`, `texts[3]`) in `TyListArg` captures repeated 3-slot patterns and makes later decompilation easier than scattered `x0/x4/x8`, `xC/x10/x14`, and text field guesses.
Converting archive symbol names to offsets into a known string blob is a useful matching tactic when direct string literals alter `.data`/relocations or string pooling behavior.

Replacing inlined JObj translation/dirty logic with official HSD setters (`HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z`, `HSD_AObjSetRate`) greatly improves readability and may match when the original calls library helpers rather than open-coded field writes.
Introducing precise small structs for archive animation data, camera state, and display state reduces pointer arithmetic where the layout evidence is strong enough.
Keeping `fn_80313BD8` static in `tylist.c` while removing it from the public header avoids exposing an internal callback when only local use is evident.
decomp_lessons: A small number of new matches can be outweighed by regressions elsewhere; here the bot showed +792 bytes overall but large drops for `un_8031305C`, `fn_80313BD8`, `un_80312BAC`, `un_8031457C`, `un_80312904`, `.data`, and `fn_8031438C`.
For UI/list code, field signedness matters: this PR changed some `u8` padding/unknowns to `s8` (`x28`, `x29D`, `x2A0`) and removed casts in places like `state->x29B`, which can affect codegen.

Typed structs improve maintainability, but using a too-typed struct can worsen matching; the diff keeps some byte-pointer operations such as `char* data = un_804A2AC0` and `memzero(data + 0x2AC, 0x18)` where exact codegen needed raw offsets.
String literal placement is part of matching. Reusing existing string tables by offset can fix functions that are otherwise semantically identical.

Library setter calls are preferable when they correspond to the original binary, but replacing expanded m2c-style field writes with setters must be checked function-by-function because it can move bytes between matches and regressions.
naming_conventions: Local inferred types used subsystem-prefixed names: `TyListArchiveAnimData`, `TyListWaitData`, `TyListCameraState`, `TyListDispEntry`, `TyListDispState`.

For partially understood structs, the PR kept offset-style member names such as `x24`, `x25`, `idx`, `x28`, `x2C`, `x30`, `x29B`, `x29C`, preserving decomp convention when semantic names were not proven.
Repeated same-kind fields were renamed to plural arrays: `links[3]`, `jobjs[3]`, and `texts[3]`.
Public prototypes in `tylist.h` favor exact pointer types (`struct TyListArg*`, `TyListState*`) instead of `void*` once layout evidence is available.
Formatting convention shown in incidental files uses a space between cast type and compound literal/function-pointer declarator, e.g. `(GXColor) { ... }` and `(u8 (*)(s32, s32, u8))`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use offsets into existing string data (`un_803FDD18 + offset`) to reproduce expected string-address references in archive loading calls.
Use HSD helper calls (`HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z`, `HSD_JObjReqAnim`, `HSD_AObjSetRate`, `HSD_JObjAnim`) instead of open-coded struct writes when the binary likely called helpers.
Sometimes cast global state to `char*` and operate by byte offsets to force exact address arithmetic and `memzero` call shapes.
Use inline helpers for repeated small loops over `TyListArg` JObjs when codegen benefits, as attempted with `un_80312E88_inline`.
Represent consecutive link/JObj/text fields as arrays to encourage indexed access and loop codegen rather than separate scalar temporaries.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code for GALE01 increased to 67.20% (+0.02%, +792 bytes).
Bot reported 2 new matches: `main/melee/ty/tylist` `un_8031263C` from 91.40% to 100.00% (+36 bytes) and `un_803124BC` from 99.29% to 100.00% (+3 bytes).
Bot reported improvements in unmatched items: `un_80313774` +693 bytes to 61.66%, `.sdata2` +94 bytes to 91.23%, `un_803147C4` +21 bytes to 85.97%, and `un_80313508` +3 bytes to 99.29%.

Bot also reported regressions: `un_8031305C` -284 bytes, `fn_80313BD8` -189 bytes, `un_80312BAC` -118 bytes, `un_8031457C` -89 bytes, `un_80312904` -71 bytes, `.data` -63 bytes, `fn_8031438C` -54 bytes, `un_80312E88` -2 bytes, and `.sdata` -1 byte.
follow_up_queries: Look for later merged PRs touching `src/melee/ty/tylist.c` that recovered `un_80313774` or reused the `TyListArg` array layout.
Search subsequent history for `TyListArchiveAnimData`, `TyListCameraState`, or `links[3]` to see whether these struct names/layouts survived review.
Investigate why direct string literals versus `un_803FDD18` offsets affected `.data` and `un_803124BC`/`un_8031263C` matching.
Compare later versions of `un_80312BAC`, `un_80312904`, and `fn_80313BD8` against this PR to identify which readability rewrites caused regressions.
Check whether the incidental formatting-only changes in gm/gr files were from automated clang-format or branch noise.
